title: She Called It Being Held Hostage. The CDC Calls It a 42-Day Quarantine.
script: Cruise tourists held in Nebraska! Angela Perryman, 47, boarded the MV Hondius for an Arctic expedition cruise. What she didn't know was that her ship was about to become ground zero for a rare hantavirus outbreak. The Andes hantavirus — one of the few that can spread from person to person — infected multiple passengers. When Perryman landed back in the US in May, federal health officials immediately quarantined her and 17 other Americans at a federal facility in Nebraska. The quarantine lasts 42 days — until June 22. The CDC says the 6-week hold is necessary. Florida's health officials say the CDC overreached. Perryman says she feels like a hostage. 10 of 18 passengers have since been allowed to finish quarantine at home. When an outbreak starts on a ship, who decides how long you have to stay?
